# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Customization Workflow with GitHub Actions (Custom Build Commands) 
on: [push] # or can be manually triggered by "workflow_dispatch" in github UI to update config map value and trigger workflow again. For manual dispatch use event: push as per requirement, for auto-triggered updates/config changes check comments above on the respective lines of code (optional).
jobs: 
   buildAndDeployJob : # Name your job based upon feature or business functionality you want to run this in parallel with other jobs. You can name it anything but remember that workflows will be named after these, so if they contain 'app' only then GitHub Actions does not know what to do!
   runs-on: ubuntu-latest # Provide the operating system required for your job here (optional).  If you want a specific version of Ubuntu run on it. Can also use docker images in this case, but requires .github/workflows directory has appropriate permission set up at runtime as well like using github packages or some other method to get image accesses
   steps: # Define the job step by步 way and add command if needed inside quotes (optional). Example - name of Step is just a string; If we want it can be an expression too. For example, 'run', "echo", etc., depends on your needs as required! Here only for naming purpose in steps
   # Commands to set up the environment –- You could use `set` commands here if needed: -x docker_login ecc $DOCKERID/$IMAGE1 . --password $PASSWORD;. /cachedrive/cache/.nvm/nvm.sh install node
   # Define and call your build command in quotes, then list the necessary dependencies as a string of comma-separated values unless otherwise required (optional). In case you want for example dockerfile: "DOCKERFILE_PATH='DockerFilePath';"  This would be ${SECRET.MYSUPERSERVERAPIKEY} and so on!
   # Here is an array of commands as a string separated by commas to pass all the requirements, for multiple calls use , followed with spaces between command: "echo Hello; exit;" or whatever needed depending upon what you need (optional). Example - name(string): hello_world ; If there are no dependencies add it in single quotes.
   # Define your steps commands and then call them within double brackets [ ] like so:[ “command” ]; Add more using comma-separated values, for multiple calls use , followed by spaces between command: "echo Hello; exit;" or whatever needed depending on what you need (optional). Example - name(string): hello_world ; If there are no dependencies add it in single quotes.
   # Define and call your post build steps inside double brackets [ ]. In the last set of commands, list them separated by commas if more then one or leave empty: [] . Add here any subsequent code to run once all previous stages/commands have been completed (optional). Example - name(string): hello_world ; If there are no dependencies add it in single quotes.
